description: According to Sabsad, on September 27, 2014, these files were put into a ZIP together. Tha Tour Pt. 1 dropped 2 days later, so these songs were likely meant to become Young Thug’s next solo project under Rich Gang until plans changed. There doesn't seem to be any order to the tracks.

name: JEFFERY [08/14/2016]
description: Tracklist posted by Thug on August 14, 2016 to his Twitter and Snapchat. At the time it was unclear if this was an actual tracklist or just a list of his inpirations, but the inclusion of "Future Swag" seems to indicate it is in fact song titles. The second track is hard to decipher due to the cursive and it seemingly not being as recognizable a name as the rest. The final track seems to be "Young Jeffrey", however "Jeffrey" looks a bit different than the handwriting up top, so it's possible it may be another word.

name: JEFFERY [08/16/2016]
description: Leaked Tumay pre-masters for a copy of "JEFFREY" from August 16, 2016. Tracks are unnumbered, indicating no tracklist had been decided yet. Although Thug posted a tracklist with names taken from his inspirations, like the final album, these tracks still have original titles. Compared to release, "Kanye West" / "Pop Man" is absent, and two cut songs are included, "Kerosene" and "Feel Like That".

name: JEFFERY [08/25/2016]
description: Tracklist shown at listening party for JEFFREY the day before its release. "Kanye West" is still titled "Pop Man", otherwise the tracklist is the same as release.

name: Slime Language 2 [Song List]
description: List of songs seen on a whiteboard being worked on in a video Wheezy posted to Instagram promoting the original Black Friday release date. Most of these are very blurry and hard to determine, so estimations of what they read are mostly based on songs known from this time period and may be mininterpreted.

tracks.name: Take It To Trial - Slime x Yak

name: Slime Language 2 [Fake Tracklist]
description: Fake tracklist given to OnThinIce and widely spread prior to the album's release. The list is proven fake by generally not resembling the final album, the only real songs being ones that had already been publicly previewed, and errors like listing "I Put A" as its fan name "King Size" and listing Swizz Beatz as a feature beacause fans thought was involved with the track since he appeared in a video teasing it, but didn't actually produce or appear on the song.

name: BUSINESS IS BUSINESS
description: Official tracklist for "BUSINESS IS BUSINESS".

tracks.name: Wit Da Racks (feat. 21 Savage, Travis Scott & Yak Gotti)

name:
BUSINESS IS BUSINESS 
(Metro's Version)
description: Official tracklist for Metro Boomin's version of "BUSINESS IS BUSINESS". When the album released he indicated this was the "true" sequence for the project in his mind, particularly with Jonesboro being the opening track. Most likely this is close to how the version he submitted looked and the label chose to reorder it for the standard release.

tracks.name: Wit Da Racks (feat. 21 Savage, Travis Scott & Yak Gotti)

name:
BUSINESS IS BUSINESS 
(3 Pack)
description: Leaked master files for "BUSINESS IS BUSINESS" reveal there were plans for a "3 Pack" EP do be released alongside the full album. The tracklist order is unknown and only two tracks are included - however, the third was likely originally present and either "Parade On Cleaveland" or "Oh U Went", both of which had their master files removed by the leaker as a slight towards Drake due to his ongoing beef with Kendrick Lamar.

name:
Love You Later 
[Listening Party Previews]
description: Four "Love You Later" tracks were previewed at the "BUSINESS IS BUSINESS" release party, indicating they were likely on the version of the album that was being prepared to release at that time.

name: UY SCUTI [V1]
description: Initial version of "UY SCUTI" uploaded to streaming services, reported by purple4matter three and a half hours prior to release. This version never actually released, as "Pardon My Back" with Lil Baby and "Invest Into You" were added to the album between the tracklist being reported and the album actually releasing. "Sad Spider" was also initially titled "Sad Slime".

tracks.name: Dreams Rarely Do Come True (feat. Mariah The Scientist)

name: UY SCUTI [V2]
description: Official release of "UY SCUTI". "Pardon My Back" was added last minute, likely having been held off initially due to either Lil Baby not clearing his verse at first or needing time to remove Future. Mariah had also been reported to have been removed from the album days prior to release, but her other feature was present on the initial upload so it's unclear why "Invest Into You" was left off at first.

tracks.name: Invest Into You (feat. Mariah The Scientist)
Dreams Rarely Do Come True (feat. Mariah The Scientist)

name: UY SCUTI [V3]
description: Updated version of "UY SCUTI" added to streaming services shortly after the album's intial release, adding "Spider or Jeffrey" with Quavo. Unknown why the track wasn't on the original version. For some reason, an otherwise identical version of the album was uploaded in addition to the oeigginal instead of the track just being added to the existing album.

tracks.name: Invest Into You (feat. Mariah The Scientist)
Dreams Rarely Do Come True (feat. Mariah The Scientist)

name: UY SCUTI (First Pressing)
description: Version of UY SCUTI included on CD and Vinyl physicals which were pressed prior to the album's completion, most likely a month or more prior to release. The tracklist differs significantly, being both nine tracks shorter and including three songs that were not included on the final album (one of which was added to the "Supernova Edition", however).

name: UY SCUTI (Vinyl Edition)
description: Official tracklist to "UY SCUTI (Vinyl Edition)", released on iTunes. Confusingly, this tracklist doesn't actually resemble the vinyl edition of the album, which included three scrapped songs and was also sequenced differently. Not really sure what this was supposed to be or why it was released.

name: UY SCUTI (Archive Edition 1)
description: Official tracklist for "UY SCUTI (Archive Edition 1)", adding "Fighting Depression" as a bonus track. Leaker sleep reported that "Fighting Depression" was wanted on the main album from the start, but sample clearance blocked it, so releasing it as a digital store bonus was the only way it could be included.

tracks.name: Invest Into You (feat. Mariah The Scientist)
Dreams Rarely Do Come True (feat. Mariah The Scientist)

name: UY SCUTI (Archive Edition 2)
description: Official tracklist for "UY SCUTI (Archive Edition 2)", adding "It's Ok To Cry" as a bonus track.

tracks.name: Invest Into You (feat. Mariah The Scientist)
Dreams Rarely Do Come True (feat. Mariah The Scientist)

name: UY SCUTI (Supernova Edition)
description: Official tracklist for "UY SCUTI (Supernova Edition)". The edition adds several 'grail' tracks to the front of the album, and only "Pussy Smell Like" is actually an "UY SCUTI" era track. Before release Thug and his team polled fans on what songs they'd like to ehar included, leading to tracks like "Rosetta Stone" and "By The Police" being added. Adin Ross suggested "Safe" when Thug appeared on his stream, but Thug seemingly misunderstood which song he meant. The "BEAUTIFUL THUGGER GIRLS"-era track was added, despite having been released officially already on YouTube, when Adin had actually meant the "Barter 7"-era song of the same name.

tracks.name: Invest Into You (feat. Mariah The Scientist)
Dreams Rarely Do Come True (feat. Mariah The Scientist)
